title: [FIRRTL][LowerToHW] Insert extra initialization for async regs
body:
This commit adds an extra initialization for async registers so
that they are set to initial values if reset signal is enabled at
an initial randomization phase. This matches SFC's behaviour.

For the following fir, 
```
circuit AsyncShift:  
  module AsyncShift:
    input clock : Clock
    input reset : AsyncReset
    output io : { flip d : UInt<1>, q : UInt<1>}

    reg sync_0 : UInt<1>, clock with :
      reset => (reset, UInt<1>("h0")) 
    reg sync_1 : UInt<1>, clock with :
      reset => (reset, UInt<1>("h0")) 
    node _sync_2_T = bits(io.d, 0, 0) 
    sync_1 <= _sync_2_T 
    sync_0 <= sync_1 
    io.q <= sync_0
```

We will get:
```verilog
 `INIT_RANDOM_PROLOG_      // foo.fir:7:5
      `ifdef RANDOMIZE_REG_INIT // foo.fir:7:5
        _RANDOM = {`RANDOM};    // foo.fir:7:5
        sync_0 = _RANDOM[0];    // foo.fir:7:5
        sync_1 = _RANDOM[1];    // foo.fir:9:5
      `endif
      if (reset) begin  // foo.fir:7:5
        sync_0 = 1'h0;  // foo.fir:7:5, :8:24
        sync_1 = 1'h0;  // foo.fir:8:24, :9:5
      end
```

close #2999.
